figures_detected.description: Cylinder block upper face with oil hole to cylinder head covered by a shop towel
Cylinder block upper face distortion measurement positions — numbered measurement directions 1 through 6
Cylinder bore measurement diagram showing X-Y (out-of-round) and A-B (taper) measurement directions
Cylinder block lower surface showing cylinder bore grade numbers and piston grade number locations, with FRONT orientation indicated

steps.text: Clean upper face of cylinder block. Cover the oil hole to cylinder head with a shop towel before removing all traces of cylinder head gasket. This will prevent blockage of the oil hole.
Measure the distortion of the upper face of cylinder block. Limit: 0.10 mm (0.0039 in)

If out of specification, resurface it. The resurfacing limit is determined by cylinder head resurfacing in engine. Amount of cylinder head resurfacing is "A". Amount of cylinder block resurfacing is "B". The maximum limit is as follows: A + B = 0.2 mm (0.008 in). Nominal cylinder block height from crankshaft center: 188.95 - 189.05 mm (7.4390 - 7.4429 in)

items.label: Maximum resurfacing limit (A + B)
items.condition: Cylinder head resurfacing (A) + cylinder block resurfacing (B)

items.label: Nominal cylinder block height from crankshaft center

steps.text: Using a bore gauge, measure cylinder bore for wear, out-of-round and taper. Standard inner diameter: 71.0 - 71.03 mm (2.7953 - 2.7965 in). Wear limit: 0.2 mm (0.008 in). Out-of-round (X-Y) limit: 0.015 mm (0.0006 in). Taper (A-B) limit: 0.010 mm (0.0004 in). If it exceeds the limit, rebore all cylinders. Replace cylinder block if necessary.
Check for scratches and seizure. If seizure is found, hone it.

content_blocks.text: If both cylinder block and piston are replaced with new ones, select piston of the same grade number punched on cylinder block lower surface.

tools_referenced.name: Bore gauge
tools_referenced.usage: Measure cylinder bore for wear, out-of-round and taper
